source.description: Six-panel raster process contact sheet generated for the June 3, 2026 rain boot with puddle archive lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet must keep one boot pose, crop, scale, and paper texture from construction through the finished sketch.

process_strategy: The sequence establishes the boot shaft, foot, sole, cuff, puddle, stripe, and reflection before the final panel only darkens keeper lines and strengthens restrained graphite and blue shading.

final_elements.name: upright boot shaft
final_elements.description: A tall rounded boot shaft leaning slightly forward.

final_elements.name: boot foot
final_elements.description: A rounded foot shape extending from the bottom of the shaft.

final_elements.name: top cuff and heel
final_elements.description: A curved top cuff plus the back heel shape.

final_elements.name: sole and toe contour
final_elements.description: A thicker sole and clearer rounded toe on the already placed foot.

final_elements.name: boot stripe
final_elements.description: A simple band stripe across the boot shaft.

final_elements.name: puddle oval
final_elements.description: A shallow oval puddle under the boot.

final_elements.name: reflection marks and soft blue shading
final_elements.description: Small puddle reflection lines plus restrained blue-gray shading on the boot and water.

frames.visible_job: Places the upright boot shaft guide and rounded foot guide.
Adds the top cuff and heel around the existing boot guide.
Clarifies the toe and adds a thicker sole to the boot foot.
Adds a stripe band on the shaft and a shallow puddle oval under the boot.
Adds reflection marks and restrained blue-gray shading to existing boot and puddle shapes.
introduces: reflection marks and soft blue shading

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies the stripe, sole, and puddle reflection, and deepens the already placed shading.

rejection_checks: No boot shaft, foot, cuff, heel, sole, stripe, puddle, reflection, or color area should first appear in the final image.
No frame may shade or darken a feature that was not already visible earlier.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same boot pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
